ai_risk_open: I worry AI models are trained on data from everything up to the present, but as society evolves, and people's attitudes change, that AI models will do a worse job of predicting actual humans. So I'd want that these models are constantly being updated (think every 6 months to a year) to be current with actual humans. I say this because I often study issues that are rapidly evolving (political and moral beliefs, intergroup attitudes) so this is more of a concern for my work.

ai_risk_open: I worry about the peer review process falling apart with AI because it's already hard to assess research when the methods are complex or idiosyncratic. Not all of us are trained in statistics in addition to political science, yet the peer review process relies on us to be broadly competent. None of us understand AI, however, (I understand it enough to get how many flaws it has for people of color, women, etc) and I worry that reviewers will not be knowledgeable enough to ask the right questions of people using AI and publishing work with the help of AI. Thus, the peer-review process may suffer -- just like it did with "big data" and machine learning models that nobody understood...
